subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: -$14.56 (-4.47% CAPR) | Chip rout deepens — FDA docs Jul 25, TSLA+GOOGL print Tue

🎯 Investment goal: $761.27 / $1,000 — $238.73 to go (76.1% there · target Jul 31)

Chip rout deepens on Moonshot AI (Kimi K3) fears — CAPR holds well above stop. Key: FDA briefing docs drop Jul 25 (7 days). AdCom Jul 29 (11 days). GTC sell $33-35 must be live in IBKR.

🌍 MACRO CONTEXT — July 18, 2026

Buffett Indicator: 237.9% 🔴 ALL-TIME EXTREME

S&P Forward P/E: 21.25x vs 10-yr avg 19.0x (+12% premium)

Trend: ↑ Rising 235.6% in June → 237.9% now — new record

Market cap is 2.38× the entire US economy — a new all-time record. Every reading above 190% has preceded a multi-year bear market; we are 48 points above that threshold.

For Abbas: Keeps stops tight. CAPR binary resolves before any macro correction materializes — AdCom trade intact. But hold cash dry and avoid deploying all $450 into new positions simultaneously with CAPR exposure.

Prior episodes: dot-com peak 2000 (148%), COVID bubble 2021 (216%). At 238% we are in genuinely uncharted territory. Source: Advisor Perspectives / Wilshire 5000, July 2026.

📰 Position News — July 18, 2026

🔵 CAPR — AdCom Jul 29 · PDUFA Aug 22

⚠️ FDA Briefing Documents: NOT YET RELEASED. Expected July 25 (7 days — 5 trading days). This is the single most important signal before the AdCom vote. FDA posts briefing docs approximately 2 business days before the committee meeting. Mark July 25 on your calendar — read the docs the moment they appear on fda.gov. If FDA is skeptical of the 2.4% LVEF improvement data: consider cutting before AdCom. If supportive: hold.

📊 Analyst activity (July 16): Roth Capital cut PT $43 → $38, maintained Buy. Reason: NS Pharma legal dispute + delayed US Deramiocel launch model pushed to 2027. Broader consensus: Strong Buy, avg PT $49.67 (6 analysts, 0 Sells). Simply Wall St: CAPR "may be 58% undervalued."

📉 Price action (July 17): CAPR closed $19.44 (range $18.66–$19.79). Down from ~$23.21 on July 3. Entry July 15 @ $20.35 → now -4.47% in 2 trading sessions. No company-specific negative news — entirely tracking the broader Nasdaq -1.4% selloff driven by Moonshot AI (Kimi K3) fears hammering the chip sector. Thesis intact.

🔔 Action: HOLD. Stop at $13 not threatened (33% below current price). Pre-set GTC sell $33-35 in IBKR — should already be live. Next decision point: July 25 (briefing docs).

Enhanced news scan ran July 18, 2026 — CAPR enhanced_news_watch: true. No 🚨 URGENT signals. No competitor CRL in DMD indication found. No FDA early action signals.

📡 Catalyst Plays — July 18, 2026

30 searches run across FDA calendar, earnings, AI/tech, options flow, short interest. Top 3 below. Sector balance enforced. Always run analyze [TICKER] before entering — data noted as incomplete where applicable.

SUPN — Supernus PharmaceuticalsFDA/PDUFA · BIOTECH

Catalyst: Centanafadine (triple reuptake inhibitor) for ADHD — PDUFA July 24, 2026 (6 days)

Probability est.: ~65% | Halal: ✅ Clean (ADHD pharmaceutical) | Mkt cap: ~$1.2-1.5B est.

Edge: Confirmed PDUFA July 24 (6 days). ADHD has robust FDA approval history. Supernus already has FDA relationship via Qelbree (viloxazine) — prior approved ADHD drug.

✅ If approval: +30-60% typical. $228 (30% portfolio) → +$68–$137 gross. Exit commission -$1. Net: +$67–$136.

💀 If CRL: -25-45% typical. $228 → -$57–$103. Net: -$58–$104 after exit commission.

⚠️ Key risk: ADHD market competitive; incomplete data on float, short interest, cash runway. Entry window closing — only 6 days to PDUFA.

Conviction: 3/5 → WATCH (data incomplete — must run analyze SUPN first)
CDNA — CareDx, Inc.POLICY/RE-RATING · MEDTECH

Catalyst: Medicare final coverage for transplant biomarker surveillance — EFFECTIVE NOW (post-event re-rating ongoing)

Mkt cap: ~$1.2B est. | Halal: ✅ Clean (medical diagnostics) | Event: Already moved +35.6% Jul 17

Edge: Structural policy win unlocks commercial runway not in prior models. Category leader in transplant surveillance. Not a one-day pop — re-rating as policy takes effect commercially in Q3/Q4.

✅ Continuation upside: Q3 commercial ramp beats estimates → further re-rating.

⚠️ Key risk: +35.6% already happened — chasing momentum. Best entry was pre-event. Different risk profile than binary plays.

Conviction: 3/5 → WATCH (run analyze CDNA for Q3 earnings setup)
MIRM — Mirum Pharmaceuticals⚫ SKIP — DATE UNCONFIRMED

Volixibat FDA approval expected July 2026 — but exact PDUFA date not confirmed from today's searches. Gate 1 failed (no confirmed date ≤45 days). Halal: ✅ Clean (rare pediatric disease drug). Run analyze MIRM to confirm date. If confirmed, re-screen next session.

💵 Cash Deployment — $448.23 available

Cash: $450.23 | Reserve: $2 | Deployable: $448.23 | Max per catalyst play: 30% × $761.27 = $228.38

Note: You are already in CAPR (41% of portfolio value). Adding SUPN would bring combined catalyst exposure to ~71% — above the 50% combined cap. Commission drag: $1 buy + $1 sell = $2 round-trip per position.

Option A — SUPN PDUFA play (Jul 24, 6 days away)

Buy ~$200 SUPN before Mon Jul 21 close. Event: PDUFA Jul 24. Upside: +30-60% if approved. Downside: -25-45% if CRL. Gross: +$60–$120 win | -$50–$90 lose. Net after $2 round-trip: +$58–$118 | -$52–$92. ⚠️ Requires analyze SUPN first — short interest, cash runway, float, and exact approval probability not yet confirmed.

Option B — Hold all cash through CAPR AdCom

Cash stays dry until Jul 29. If CAPR wins (+$231 net), you have $761+ to redeploy into next high-conviction setup with full capital. If CAPR loses (-$116 net), you still have $450 — strong position to rebuild. Two binary events in parallel = 71% catalyst exposure, above the 50% portfolio cap in the playbook.

📌 Recommended: Option B. Hold cash through CAPR AdCom. Risk cap breach + incomplete SUPN data both argue against adding now. Your call.

📊 Risk Scores · 🔄 Rotation

CAPR | 75/100 | EXTREME | Pre-revenue binary event; AdCom 11d; prior CRL; all value in single event

Rotation: None recommended. Anti-rotation rule: within 11 days of catalyst, let the stop work. CAPR strategy is already decided — hold to AdCom, sell the event. No pre-emptive exits. IBKR Intelligence unavailable in routine mode — run daily check in chat for sector allocation and position themes.

📚 Today you learned — Concept 2 of 30 (the missing one)

EV/EBITDA

Forward P/E ignores debt and cash — EV/EBITDA fixes both. Enterprise Value (EV) = market cap + total debt − cash. EBITDA = earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization. The ratio tells you what an acquirer would actually pay, normalized for capital structure. A company with $10B market cap and $5B cash is fundamentally cheaper than one with $10B cap and $5B in debt — P/E misses this entirely; EV/EBITDA catches it. For CAPR right now: they're pre-revenue, so neither metric applies — analysts instead use probability-weighted NPV models for binary biotech. But when you rotate out of CAPR post-AdCom into a profitable name — MSFT (~27x EV/EBITDA), ISRG (~48x, now -10%), or a small-cap re-rating play like CDNA — this is the number you'll compare. Semis: NVDA trades ~32x EV/EBITDA, premium for a monopolist growing 65%+ but within historical range.

Also today: Net cash position — when a company's cash exceeds total debt (EV < market cap), such as Apple or Alphabet, the net cash actually reduces your effective purchase price. Their EV is lower than their market cap — a hidden margin of safety.

Research output, not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Verify halal compliance quarterly on Zoya or Musaffa. Generated July 18, 2026, 06:00 Kuwait time (UTC+3).
